tweet.text:
As agentic systems become more autonomous, their biggest failure mode isn't model capability - it's context. 

Agents struggle with accuracy, consistency, and reasoning when memory is shallow or unstructured. Knowledge graphs provide a powerful way to represent relationships, state, and history, enabling agents to reason over how things connect, not just what text is retrieved.

Nim has three levels of memory. He doesn't just retrieve info. He reasons over relationships.
